ENGLAND: F EDI S F LON - NTH

messages.message: Turkey — I speak plainly. Austria is squeezed on all sides and must secure its rear to survive. The Balkans will be the pressure point this year: Russia can push through Galicia into Rumania/Serbia and threaten our Budapest center; Italy's fleet and army can threaten Trieste; and any Ottoman movement into the Aegean or through the Dardanelles could leave our southern flank exposed. I propose the following clear, practical arrangement: you avoid any move into the Adriatic (no convoying to Albania or support to Albania) and I will keep my fleet in Trieste/Adriatic non-aggressive toward your Aegean positions. If Russia pushes against me, I will expect you to tie down a Russian fleet/army in the Black Sea region (Sevastopol/Ankara area) or at least threaten a Russian southern flank so Moscow must divert resources. This is about mutual survival, not grand aims. Can you commit to non-aggression in the Adriatic and at least consider contesting Sevastopol/Black Sea if Russia presses west?

France — England here. We start S1901 with mutual interest in keeping the Channel and North Sea safe for both our fleets. I’m prepared to move cautiously: F LON→NTH (supported by F EDI) and keep A LVP available to secure the northwestern coast. If you keep F BRE and A PAR away from a plunge into the North Sea (no move into NTH, NWG, or ENG this turn), I will not contest Brest and will focus my fleet posture northward to deter Germany. I can also support you diplomatically against any German flank moves. Note: my assessment projects Germany is somewhat likely to try a northern naval push (storyworld forecast confidence ~60%); prudent for us to avoid accidental conflict. Agree?

Germany — England seeks stability in the North Sea. I will move F LON→NTH supported by F EDI, and keep A LVP around to protect the northwest. I prefer no surprise fleet rush through the NTH→DEN line this phase. If you refrain from moving F KIE into DEN or NTH this turn, I will not press to take Belgian centers or challenge your Kiel/Berlin flank. This keeps channels open and reduces risk of naval escalation. Thoughts?

Russia — England to you: I will adopt a cautious maritime posture this turn to avoid a two-front naval squeeze. Expect F LON to NTH and F EDI to hold or support northward movements. I am open to coordination if you plan Baltic or Norwegian moves — a neutral channel stance benefits both of us. Small forecast note I share for transparency: my modelling suggests a ~60% chance of northern naval pressure from Germany this phase; if you see KIE→DEN or KIE→NTH on your side, tell me and I’ll temper my moves to prevent accidental clash.

Italy — England here. No aggression intended toward you. I will focus on safeguarding the Channel and North Sea. If you want neutrality guaranteed from me, I will refrain from moves toward the Bay of Biscay and will not support France against you. In return, I’d ask you avoid opening an alliance against France or provisioning German sea access through Tyrrhenian commitments that could free German fleets north. A quiet Mediterranean allows me to keep naval leverage elsewhere.

Austria — England. I’m offering an open line: I have no designs on you and will not interfere in the Adriatic. My immediate concern is balancing Germany on the continent and keeping sea lanes safe. I passed you my forecast artifact (see provenance) suggesting a moderate chance of northern aggression by Germany; if Germany presses north, I may need flexibility to shift support toward France. Can we stay neutral to each other this year?

Turkey — England. I won’t intrude into the Mediterranean or Aegean while you hold your centers. My priority is a strong naval stance in the North Sea; I prefer no entangling commitments in the east. If you require assurances versus Russian moves out of Sevastopol, I’m willing to offer diplomatic support but not fleets. Confirm neutrality and we avoid friction.

Forecast artifact for transparency: England projects a 60% likelihood of northern naval aggression (Germany) in S1901. I share this to encourage cautious fleet placement by neighbors and avoid accidental clashes in the Channel/North Sea. I favor coordinated restraint: France keep BRE clear of NTH/ENG, Germany avoid KIE→NTH or DEN, and Russia notify if STP/SC or SEV plans threaten northern routing. England will posture defensively (NTH focus) in return.

H.M. Government — Salut. I write as a neighbour concerned with German intent in the north. I will keep Brest secure and do not intend an immediate naval dash to the Channel that would threaten your home — rather, I seek a clear understanding: if I move F BRE to ENG or MAO this turn, will you refrain from contesting the Channel and instead focus your fleets against Germany? A quiet Channel benefits both of us (keeps Germany bottled on the continent) and lets us cooperate against Berlin. I can offer peaceful maritime intentions in exchange for your not supporting a German fleet into BEL/ENG waters. Thoughts?

Herr Kollege — France prefers clarity to surprises. I will move to secure my southern and western approaches. I have no desire for early large-scale war with you, but I will respond firmly to any aggression. If you concentrate on attacking me through the west (Burgundy/Picardy), you risk England joining against you. Consider redirecting forces eastward or negotiating a neutral line through Belgium. If you refrain from moving into BEL and threaten only via MUN/KIE, I will avoid rash moves into the Channel and may even leave your eastern flank less pressured. Your forecast: are you leaning at all west (Belgium) or east (Alsace/neutral Poland theatre)?

England — Germany here. We begin S1901 with full home defense but seek clarity on your intentions in the north. Russia's fleet at StP and England's fleets create a two‑front risk for us unless we coordinate. I propose: F KIE → DEN and A BER hold (or F KIE hold and A MUN support A BER hold) this season to secure Baltic access while leaving the North Sea open for a cooperative approach. If you intend to contest Denmark or the North Sea, tell me now. Otherwise we can agree to avoid immediate naval clashes — Germany will not open on England if you do not move into DEN/HOL/KIE. Confirm your planned moves and any interest in a limited Anglo‑German understanding for 1901.

Russia — Germany. With your strong presence in Warsaw and StP, we face a credible two‑front exposure. I want to avoid accidental conflict. My immediate priority is to secure the Baltic approaches and Munich lines. I propose mutual nonaggression: I will not advance into Galicia/PRU if you refrain from moving into Prussia or Kiel this turn. Specifically: A BER will hold or move to PRU only if you threaten WAR/PRU; F KIE may move to BAL/DEN only with explicit notice. Can we agree to notify each other of any moves toward PRU/BER/WAR to avoid clashes?

Friend in Vienna — greetings. I hope Trieste and your positions look stable for you. I plan to keep my fleet quietly in the Tyrrhenian/Adriatic tempo for now. I value a calm Adriatic so we can both avoid costly early clashes. If you prefer, I will keep Venice passive (hold) or move into Tyrolia/Trento only with clear coordination. Would you accept a mutual understanding: no Austrian move into the northern Adriatic (Trieste↔Adriatic) if I do not press on your southern flank from Venice? That preserves our home centers and gives us bargaining power later. I remain flexible — if your forecast changes, tell me and we can pivot.

To Constantinople — salutations. I am minded to keep the Mediterranean lanes open and avoid an immediate clash with you. I will likely probe into the Ionian or Tyrrhenian but only if it suits both our interests. Would you be open to a neutral/no-aggression understanding for the Adriatic/Ionian this phase so we can both consolidate? If you plan to rush the Balkans, warn me and I will adjust toward Albania/Apulia to protect my flank. Otherwise, let us keep options open and trade forecasts.

Friend in Constantinople — Russia proposes calm and clear lines for 1901. We will not move from Sevastopol into the Black Sea aggressively if you keep Ankara and Constantinople secure and avoid moving into Armenia. I plan to push only a coast or Rumania move to secure my southern flank. In return, could you keep your fleets away from the southern Black Sea (Bulgarian coast and south of Constantinople) and agree neutrality in Armenia for this year? This allows us both breathing room and opens possibility of mutually useful cooperation vs Austria or the Ottomans later.

Vienna — Russia prefers a stable northern-southern balance. I am prepared to leave Galicia alone if Austria avoids pressing into Ukraine and Rumania this turn. I will not support any Austrian move into Galicia if you agree to keep Burgas/Bulgaria and Rumania neutral between us for now. If you accept, I will move conservatively from Warsaw toward LVN or Galicia in ways that do not threaten Vienna directly. A quiet border benefits us both.

Berlin — Russia is not seeking an early clash on the Prussian border. I will keep my army posture defensive in Warsaw and Moscow. If Germany likewise avoids moves into Prussia/Prussia-coast (LVN/PRU) heading east, we can each focus elsewhere. I am open to a nonaggression understanding for the coming year, and to coordinate against any sudden Austrian or Ottoman ambitions should they arise.

Russia — Constantinople to you in frank, calm terms. I value secure borders and stable straits. My fleet in the Black Sea (Sevastopol) is a mutual concern: neither of us benefits from sudden clashes that could close the Straits or draw Austria or others in. I propose a simple, defensive understanding for 1901: I will keep my fleet in AnK/BLA movements cautious and avoid aggressive moves toward Sevastopol if you avoid moves into ARM or aggressive pushes from SEV toward CON/ANK. If you are willing, let's pledge to respect a Black Sea buffer: no fleet advances into each other's immediate coastal zones (you hold SEV/STP, I hold CON/ANK/SMY). This guarantees the Bosphorus remains open and avoids escalation. If you accept, I will publicly state I have no designs on SEV and will refrain from moving into ARM this spring. Thoughts?

Austria — I seek steady borders in the Balkans. Your units in Trieste and Vienna are close to the Balkans; I prefer a calm frontier. I'm prepared to leave Bulgaria and Rumania as neutral buffer zones so long as you do not push forces through the Adriatic or into BUL/RUM via Serbia. A simple DMZ in the southern Balkans (BUL/RUM/SER off-limits to both of us) would secure our flanks and let us focus on protecting the Straits. Are you willing to agree to such a neutral Balkan belt for 1901?

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I spoke candidly with neighbors about Austria's vulnerability in the Balkans and the need for clear, enforceable restraint there. Turkey agreed to a southern Balkan buffer (BUL/RUM/SER off-limits) and asked Austria to avoid moves through the Adriatic; Turkey also signaled willingness to contest Sevastopol/Black Sea if Russia presses west. Italy offered to keep Venice passive or hold in the Adriatic/Tyrrhenian if Austria avoids aggressive northern Adriatic moves; I promised restraint toward the Adriatic. England declared no designs on Austria and will not interfere in the Adriatic; England shared a forecast of possible German northern naval activity and asked for continental restraint in return. Russia proposed leaving Galicia alone if Austria avoids moves into Ukraine and Rumania, and offered conservative moves from Warsaw that would not threaten Vienna directly, conditioned on Bulgaria/Rumania neutrality. In short: a practical DMZ in southern Balkans (BUL/RUM/SER) backed by Turkey/Russia mutual restraint, Italian non-aggression from Venice, and English neutrality. These are explicit, reciprocal understandings to buy Austria time against immediate multi-front pressure.
Intent: S1901M orders intent: Hold Vienna (A VIE H) to keep the core safe and signal non-aggression; Hold Trieste (F TRI H) to respect Adriatic non-aggression commitments with Turkey and Italy while projecting a defensive posture in the southern approaches; Move Budapest cautiously—intend to hold Budapest (A BUD H) this phase rather than advance into Galicia/Serbia/Rumania to honor the DMZ agreement with Turkey and Russia. I will fulfill these commitments. If Russia or Turkey violates the agreed Balkan neutrality (moves into BUL/RUM/SER or threats toward BUD/TRI), I will immediately revise to contest Galicia/Serbia or seek Italian

[S1901M] Turkey and Russia honored the Balkan détente: Turkey held and Russia moved Sevastopol→RUM, but crucially left Galicia alone; both respected the implicit BUL/RUM/SER buffer for now. Italy kept Venice passive as promised. England moved fleets north—no threat to Austria but signals German naval activity. No one attacked my units; I kept VIE, BUD, TRI held and honoured my restraint. No betrayals this turn; promises mostly kept.

Impact: short-term survival secured; Russia’s presence in Rumania is a warning—if it presses west I'll need to contest Galicia quickly. Trust rankings: Turkey/Italy/England = reliable; Russia = conditionally reliable while Galicia untouched. Lesson: maintain clear, documented DMZs and ask for written reciprocal moves next phase. Prepare contingency to reinforce Galicia and Serbia if Russian/Turkish posture shifts.

goals: Protect English home centers (LON, EDI, LVP) and maintain naval superiority in the North Sea/Channel
Prevent a single continental hegemon—use naval leverage and flexible diplomacy to shape coalitions
Avoid entanglement in the Mediterranean/Aegean; secure neutrality of Italy, Austria, and Turkey for 1901
Maintain tacit cooperation with France to keep the Channel quiet and coordinate against German northern naval moves
Use forecasts about fleet movement and Channel/North Sea risk to influence allies' and rivals' orders; stay ready to defend if Germany breaches non‑aggression
full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England has communicated a defensive, North Sea‑focused posture and shared a forecast indicating a ~60% chance of German northern naval action to encourage restraint. France agreed to keep Brest from an immediate naval dash into the Channel/North Sea in exchange for England not contesting Brest and focusing north. Germany proposed limited coastal/baltic moves (KIE→DEN or hold) and requested that England avoid opening on Denmark/North Sea; England signaled intention to move F LON→NTH supported by F EDI and to avoid aggressive continental moves if Germany keeps KIE out of NTH/DEN. Russia was informed of England's northward naval posture and asked to notify if StP/SC or Sevastopol moves threaten northern routing; England offered coordination. Italy, Austria, and Turkey were each offered neutrality and non‑interference: England will avoid Mediterranean/Aegean moves and not support aggression toward those powers in exchange for their staying neutral toward England this year. Overall outcome: tacit Anglo‑French understanding to keep Channel quiet, a tentative Anglo‑German non‑aggression overture in the North Sea contingent on German restraint, and broad offers of neutrality to southern/eastern powers to concentrate naval leverage northwards.
Intent: Planned orders for S1901M: F LON → NTH, supported by F EDI → NTH (or F EDI support F LON→NTH); A LVP hold (or A LVP move to YOR only if diplomacy requires coastal defense). I intend to fulfil these commitments: I will not contest Brest or support moves against France nor will I direct fleets into the Mediterranean. If Germany moves F KIE→DEN or holds KIE and avoids NTH, I will maintain the defensive North Sea posture and seek to deter German ingress into HOL/ENG. If signs appear of German aggression into NTH/ENG or coordination with Russia threatens home waters, I will adjust support orders to defend home centers and notify allies. Key contingency: if France breaks agreement by moving F BRE toward ENG/NTH, I will refuse the non‑contest and prioritize defending LON/EDIN and contesting ENG/MAO as necessary.

[S1901M] S1901M — The northward posture paid off. I successfully moved LON→NTH supported by EDI as promised; Germany honored its tacit restraint by moving KIE→DEN rather than into the North Sea. France respected our understanding by not pushing Brest toward the Channel. No power openly betrayed agreements this month; I did not break my commitments. Collaboration with France and Germany held, giving England temporary naval freedom in the North Sea and clear lanes for patrol and deterrence. Surprise: Russia consolidated in the Baltic (STP→LVN) and pushed Sevastopol→RUM — a reminder Russia may contest northern routes indirectly. Impact: home centers secure for now; keep watching Kie/Den moves and Franco-Russian interactions. Lesson: sustain diplomatic signals and timely support declarations; offer Italy/Austria/Turkey continued neutrality to avoid Mediterranean distractions.

[S1901M] S1901M — The northward posture paid off. I successfully moved LON→NTH supported by EDI as promised; Germany honored its tacit restraint by moving KIE→DEN rather than into the North Sea. France respected our understanding by not pushing Brest toward the Channel. No power openly betrayed agreements this month; I did not break my commitments. full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England and I have a tentative maritime non‑aggression understanding: I will keep Brest out of an immediate Channel plunge and avoid moves into NTH/ENG/NWG if England postures north (F LON→NTH supported by F EDI, A LVP held). England will focus fleet posture northward to deter Germany and refrain from contesting Brest. With Germany I signaled a preference for Western restraint: I warned that any German push through BEL/PIC risks drawing English support; I proposed a de facto neutral line through Belgium in exchange for Germany avoiding western aggression. No firm commitment from Germany yet. No other powers have committed to or threatened France directly this phase. Key bargaining points to remember for orders: England expects no French fleet move into the Channel; England will in turn avoid contesting Brest and will posture north to contain Germany; warn Germany that western moves invite Anglo‑French reaction and that I will secure southern approaches first.
Intent: S1901M orders: execute cautious

[S1901M] England honored our maritime understanding: LON→NTH and EDI support north, and I held Brest back by moving GAS in place — good discipline. I moved PAR→PIC and MAR→SPA to secure southern approaches; no German westward probing occurred — Germany kept quiet in BER/MUN while shifting KIE→DEN. No betrayals from England or Germany; I committed to restraint and kept it. Surprise: Germany’s fleet to DEN hints at Baltic/Scandi intent, not immediate western pressure. Impact: our western front remains stable and England is marginally reliable for now; southern Spain move strengthens my hold on Iberian buffer. Lesson: maintain clear signals to England and use Iberian positions to bargain; watch German naval posture for a northern flanking plan and be ready to remind Berlin that any push through BEL/PIC will invite Anglo‑French reaction.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England requested restraint in the North Sea; offered to hold off Belgian/Channel pressure if Germany avoids Denmark/North Sea this turn. England will move F LON->NTH supported by F EDI and keep A LVP defensive. Germany proposed securing Baltic access (DEN or BAL) while avoiding immediate naval clash; asked England to confirm. France signaled no desire for early war and prefers Germany avoid Belgium; will refrain from Channel moves if Germany does not threaten BEL. Russia agreed to a defensive Warsaw/Moscow posture and is open to a mutual nonaggression understanding; will avoid moves into Prussian borders if Germany avoids eastward probes. No binding commitments from Austria, Italy, or Turkey yet. Overall: verbal understandings with England (north restraint), France (no westward aggression/avoid BEL), and Russia (mutual nonaggression on Prussia/Warsaw). England and Russia expressed these intentions clearly; Germany committed to reciprocal restraint contingent on their moves.
Intent: I intend to prioritize security on both fronts while honoring verbal agreements: F KIE -> DEN to secure Baltic approach (informing England this move is for BAL/DEN access, not aggression), A BER hold to protect Berlin, A MUN hold to guard southern border and Austria. I will inform England and Russia immediately of these moves to lock in the understanding; I intend to fulfill the promise not to move into BEL, HOL, or NTH this turn and not to advance toward WAR/PRU beyond defensive holds. If England or Russia violate the understanding during adjudication, I will reassess in the next phase.

[S1901M] I held Berlin and Munich and sent the fleet from Kiel to occupy Denmark as promised — England honored our verbal restraint, moving LON->NTH and supporting from EDI rather than contesting Baltic access. Russia kept its defensive posture in Warsaw/Moscow and moved north and south without probing Prussia; France refrained from Belgium and instead advanced in the west. No power violated our assurances; we did not break promises. Collaboration with England and Russia succeeded and secured our northern and eastern flank this turn. Surprise: none significant — disciplined execution by neighbors. Impact: Baltic access gained, two-front risk reduced for now. Trustworthiness: England and Russia reliable this phase; France passive. Lesson: continue communicating intent, convert verbal understandings into reciprocal visible moves to lock in cooperation.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England has signaled defensive northern naval focus and offered neutrality toward Italy in exchange for Italian non-aggression versus France and no Tyrrhenian support that would free German northern fleets. I told Austria I'd keep Venice passive / avoid pressing Trieste in return for Austrian restraint in the northern Adriatic (no advance into the northern Adriatic or Trieste↔Adriatic moves). To Turkey I proposed a temporary neutral/no-agreement for the Adriatic/Ionian this phase and asked to be warned if Turkey rushes the Balkans; I offered to adjust toward Albania/Apulia only if Turkey goes aggressive. No firm commitments were extracted beyond tacit non-aggression understandings: England — neutrality pledge (no moves into Bay of Biscay, no support against Italy); Austria — mutual Adriatic restraint if I hold Venice; Turkey — request for warning and mutual neutrality in Adriatic/Ionian this phase. All agreements are conditional and flexible; I retain option to pivot if forecasts change or if others break understandings.
Intent: S1901M orders will prioritize a safe consolidation and signaling of non-aggression while keeping options open. Planned moves (subject to last-minute pivot): F NAP -> TYR (Tyrrhenian Sea) to show naval presence but not invade Sicily; A VEN -> VIE (hold or move to TYR if diplomacy requires) OR A VEN H to demonstrate passivity per Austria understanding; A ROM -> APU (move to Apulia) to cover the Ionian/Ionian approaches and threaten Albania if Turkey moves. I intend to fulfill the passive Venice understanding by not moving into Trieste or the northern Adriatic. If Austria explicitly moves toward TRI/ADR in adjudication, I will pivot F NAP to SIC or support APU->TUN to secure southern options. If England/Germany forecasts indicate northern naval

[S1901M] England kept its neutrality pledge in practice—fleets moved north, no interference with Italy. Austria honored our tacit understanding: Vienna and Trieste held, no push into the northern Adriatic. Turkey stayed quiet as asked. I kept Venice passive and moved Rome to Apulia while sending Naples into the Ionian to show presence without escalation. No promises were broken by others, nor by me. The surprise was the overall calm—everyone consolidated rather than grabbed contested seas; this preserves maneuver room. Impact: Italy maintains a safe coastal triangle (Nap/Apu/Ion) and diplomatic credibility with Austria and England. Lesson: conditional, low-risk signals paid off—continue using passive holds plus subtle naval posts to buy time and pivot if Austria or Turkey shows aggression.

Planned moves (subject to last-minute pivot): F NAP -> TYR (Tyrrhenian Sea) to show naval presence but not invade Sicily; A VEN -> VIE (hold or move to TYR if diplomacy requires) OR A VEN H to demonstrate passivity per Austria understanding; A ROM -> APU (move to Apulia) to cover the Ionian/Ionian approaches and threaten Albania if Turkey moves. I intend to fulfill the passive Venice understanding by not moving into Trieste or the northern Adriatic. If Austria explicitly moves toward TRI/ADR in adjudication, I will pivot F NAP to SIC or support APU->TUN to secure southern options. If England/Germany forecasts indicate northern naval

[S1901M] England kept its neutrality pledge in practice—fleets moved north, no interference with Italy. Austria honored our tacit understanding: Vienna and Trieste held, no push into the northern Adriatic. Turkey stayed quiet as asked. I kept Venice passive and moved Rome to Apulia while sending Naples into the Ionian to show presence without escalation. No promises were broken by others, nor by me. The surprise was the overall calm—everyone consolidated rather than grabbed contested seas; this preserves maneuver room. Impact: Italy maintains a safe coastal triangle (Nap/Apu/Ion) and diplomatic credibility with Austria and England. Lesson: conditional, low-risk signals paid off—continue using passive holds plus subtle naval posts to buy time and pivot if Austria or Turkey shows aggression.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Agreements reached: (1) With England: reciprocal cautious naval posture—England will posture defensively (F LON→NTH, F EDI hold/support north) and is open to coordinating to avoid accidental clashes in the North Sea/Baltic; England asked to be warned if Russia moves STP/SC or SEV toward northern routings. (2) With Germany: mutual non‑aggression on the Prussian/Polish front for this year—Russia will keep Warsaw/Moscow defensive and not push into Prussia/Kiel if Germany refrains from moving into Galicia/PRU/Prussia‑coast; both sides will notify each other of any moves toward WAR/PRU/BER. (3) With Turkey: a Black Sea buffer understanding—Russia will avoid aggressive SEV→CON/ANK/ARM moves and Turkey will keep fleets/armies from pressing into SEV or Armenia; both pledge to respect immediate coastal zones to keep the Straits open. (4) With Austria: tacit local non‑aggression—Russia will refrain from supporting Austrian moves into Galicia and asks Austria to avoid pressing into Ukraine/Rumania/Bulgarian border in return. Overall tone: defensive, multi‑front deconfliction, and commitment to notify neighbors of any threatening moves to avoid accidental escalations.
Intent: Spring 1901 Military Intentions (I intend to carry out these moves and agreements): - A WAR hold or move cautious: Primary plan A WAR hold to signal non‑aggression toward Germany/Austria; secondary contingency A WAR→GAL only if Austria explicitly vacates Galicia or breaks agreement. I do not intend unconditional WAR→PRU/KIE. - A MOS hold to preserve central strength and avoid provoking Germany. - F STP/SC move to BOT or hold? I will move

[S1901M] Spring 1901 review: England and I honored our cautious naval understanding — my StP→LVN and England’s NTH posture were clean and reassuring. Turkey kept to the Black Sea buffer; Sevastopol’s fleet moved to Rum successfully without Turkish interference. Germany held on the Prussian front as agreed; Warsaw and Moscow remained defensive per our pledge. Austria and Italy stayed quiet. No betrayals detected this turn; I kept my promises. Impact: my northern flank is more secure with LVN forward and British naval restraint, and the southern buffer persists, freeing me to bide time. Trust levels: England and Turkey reliable so far; Germany cautious but compliant. Lesson: maintain clear notifications when probing Galicia or Polish borders to preserve these tacit non‑aggression ties.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I sought stable, defensible borders and secure control of the Straits. Austria asked me to avoid the Adriatic and to contest Russian pressure in the Black Sea if Russia attacks; I counter-proposed a DMZ of BUL/RUM/SER to keep the southern Balkans neutral. England promises to avoid the Mediterranean/Aegean while I hold my centers and offered diplomatic support vs. Russia if needed. Italy prefers no aggression in the Adriatic/Ionian this phase and is open to a non-aggression understanding. Russia and I agreed a defensive Black Sea understanding for 1901: I will avoid aggressive moves into ARM and immediate advances on SEV, and Russia will avoid moves from SEV toward CON/ANK and avoid southern Black Sea/coastal advances that threaten the Straits. Overall: tentative understandings with Austria (no Adriatic from me; DMZ proposal for BUL/RUM/SER), England (neutrality), Italy (Adriatic/Ionian non-aggression), and Russia (Black Sea buffer/no hostile moves vs CON/ANK/ARM). No binding commitments received from Austria on the DMZ yet; Austria expects me to threaten Sevastopol if Russia attacks it.
Intent: I intend to act cautiously and defensively in S1901M to protect the Straits and preserve the status quo. Specific intended orders I will aim to issue (and will honor the above agreements): 1) F ANK — hold (maintain presence guarding the Bosphorus/Black Sea approaches). 2) A CON — hold (signal non-aggression toward ARM and conserve forces to defend Constantinople). 3) A SMY — move to ARM or hold depending on Russian public intent: if Russia visibly moves A MOS→ARM or F SEV→BLA, use A SMY→ARM to threaten and deter; otherwise A

[S1901M] Russia honored our Black Sea understanding this turn: F SEV→RUM was cautious coastal pressure but no move toward CON/ANK or ARM, and F STP→LVN shows northern focus. I held ANK/CON/SMY as promised—no betrayals from us. No Austrian reply on the proposed BUL/RUM/SER DMZ; Austria held and did not press the Adriatic, consistent with its message but noncommittal on the DMZ. England moved fleets north and stayed out of the Mediterranean as agreed. No surprises in orders; everyone largely conservative.

Impact: Our Straits remain secure and Russia appears restrained for now, reducing immediate threat. Austria’s silence on the DMZ keeps southern Balkans uncertain—must press diplomatically for a binding neutral zone. Lesson: convert verbal assurances into clearer, reciprocal written limits, and keep forces ready to counter any swift Russian pivot to the south.

support.order: F EDI S F LON - NTH

goals: Protect English home centers (LON, EDI, LVP) and maintain naval superiority in the North Sea/Channel
Prevent a single continental hegemon—use naval leverage and flexible diplomacy to shape coalitions
Avoid entanglement in the Mediterranean/Aegean; secure neutrality of Italy, Austria, and Turkey for 1901
Maintain tacit cooperation with France to keep the Channel quiet and coordinate against German northern naval moves
Use forecasts about fleet movement and Channel/North Sea risk to influence allies' and rivals' orders; stay ready to defend if Germany breaches non‑aggression
